hazards.name: Structural Collapse During Demolition
hazards.description: Uncontrolled collapse of the existing bridge structure during demolition activities, potentially affecting workers, equipment, and the waterway below.
hazards.topEventDescription: Load-bearing elements fail in an uncontrolled manner during demolition sequencing, causing partial or complete collapse outside the planned drop zone.

threats.name: Incorrect Demolition Sequence
threats.description: Structural elements removed in the wrong order, causing unexpected load transfer and progressive failure of remaining sections.

barriers.name: Structural Engineering Assessment
barriers.description: Independent structural engineer reviews and approves the demolition sequence plan, including load path analysis for each removal stage.

barriers.name: Real-Time Structural Monitoring
barriers.description: Tilt sensors, strain gauges, and crack monitors installed at critical points with automated alerts when thresholds are exceeded.

threats.name: Unknown Structural Deterioration
threats.description: Hidden corrosion, concrete spalling, or rebar degradation from 60 years of service not identified in pre-demolition surveys.

barriers.name: Comprehensive Pre-Demolition Survey
barriers.description: Non-destructive testing (GPR, ultrasonic) and core sampling at critical structural joints before demolition begins.

consequences.name: Worker Fatalities or Serious Injuries
consequences.description: Personnel struck by falling structural elements, debris, or caught in collapse zone.

barriers.name: Exclusion Zones and Access Control
barriers.description: Physical barriers and GPS-tracked personnel exclusion zones enforced around active demolition areas with 50m buffer beyond calculated debris fall radius.

barriers.name: Emergency Response Plan
barriers.description: On-site emergency response team with rescue equipment, first aid, and direct hospital communication. Drill conducted weekly.

consequences.name: Waterway Obstruction
consequences.description: Debris falls into the commercial waterway, blocking vessel traffic and potentially damaging passing vessels.

barriers.name: Marine Traffic Management
barriers.description: Harbour master coordination with vessel exclusion windows during critical demolition lifts. Guard boats stationed upstream and downstream.

hazards.name: Deep Piling Operations Near Existing Infrastructure
hazards.description: 30-metre deep piling for new bridge foundations, conducted adjacent to live utilities, the active waterway, and residential properties.
hazards.topEventDescription: Piles deviate from alignment or piling vibrations cause ground settlement affecting adjacent structures and underground utilities.

threats.name: Encountering Unknown Underground Services
threats.description: Uncharted utilities, old bridge foundations, or abandoned infrastructure encountered during piling that are not shown on service drawings.

barriers.name: Ground Penetrating Radar Survey
barriers.description: Full GPR survey of all piling locations to 35m depth, cross-referenced with utility provider records and historical bridge drawings.

barriers.name: Trial Holes and Soft Dig
barriers.description: Vacuum excavation trial holes at each pile location before piling rig mobilisation to physically confirm ground conditions.

threats.name: Excessive Vibration from Piling
threats.description: Impact or vibratory piling transmits ground vibrations exceeding safe limits to adjacent residential buildings and buried utilities.

barriers.name: Vibration Monitoring and Limits
barriers.description: Continuous vibration monitors on nearest residential properties with automatic piling shutdown if PPV exceeds 5mm/s.

consequences.name: Damage to Residential Properties
consequences.description: Settlement or vibration damage to adjacent residential buildings, including cracking, structural movement, or utility disruption.

barriers.name: Pre-Condition Survey and Settlement Monitoring
barriers.description: Detailed building condition surveys before works commence. Optical level monitoring points on all properties within 50m, read daily.

consequences.name: Utility Strike and Service Disruption
consequences.description: Damage to gas, water, electricity, or telecoms infrastructure causing service outages to the residential area.

barriers.name: Utility Protection Agreements
barriers.description: Formal protection agreements with each utility provider. Asset protection measures (concrete slabbing, hand dig zones) implemented before piling.
